GKN's RM12 upgrade extends Gripen C/D operational viability while establishing a sustainable engine support model for legacy combat aircraft fleets.

How Much Does It Cost To Upgrade To Premium Economy On US Airlines?

A closer look at the true cost of upgrading to premium economy on major US carriers.

Premium economy upgrade costs vary significantly by carrier and route, ranging from $50-$500+, making ancillary revenue optimization a key differentiator in competitive domestic markets.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Airbus Snub: Why Finnair Just Chose Embraer's E2 Over The A220

Finnair's bold move to order 18 Embraer E195-E2 jets and 12 used Airbus A320/A321s signals a major shift in the airline's short-haul strategy.

Finnair's preference for Embraer's newer E2 over Airbus's modern A220 indicates regional carriers are prioritizing cabin density and used-aircraft availability over technological advancement.

Safety Mar 24, 2026

Tropical Cyclone Narelle Crosses Australia

The powerful storm lashed the northern edge of the continent with damaging winds and drenching rain as it made landfall multiple times.

Cyclone Narelle's multiple landfall pattern and intense wind speeds require airlines operating in Australian northern regions to implement extended ground stops and reassess dispatch criteria for the affected period.

See NASA →

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Effective SMS relies on psychological safety that encourages non-punitive reporting, transforming organizational culture from blame-focused to learning-focused systems.

Boeing 787 Range Boost: FAA Approves 400 Extra Miles For The Dreamliner

Extended range capability reduces the need for intermediate stops on long-haul routes, improving schedule flexibility and reducing operational costs for airlines operating 787s globally.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Brussels Airlines BRU-JFK: distinctly Belgian and rather decadent

Brussels Airlines, part of the Lufthansa Group, is an odd duck. Despite operating in a unique market, Africa, and being the main airline serving the de facto political capital of Europe, its long-haul fleet comprises ageing Airbus A330s with a variety of business class cabins. While some of the t...

Brussels Airlines' fragmented A330 cabin configurations across its Africa-focused long-haul network create operational complexity and inconsistent customer experience that rivals should avoid when managing inherited fleets.

How Much Does An Airbus A350-900 Cost In 2026?

Despite its high list price, the A350-900's presence on the global stage is massive.

Airlines typically negotiate 40-50% discounts off Airbus's $445M list price for A350-900s, making actual acquisition costs significantly lower than published figures suggest.

Safety Mar 24, 2026

NTSB Team Still En Route To LaGuardia: Delayed By TSA Lines

The NTSB is undertaking a complete walking inspection of the LaGuardia crash site, facing a tremendous amount of debris strewn across the runway and taxiways.

TSA screening delays are hampering accident investigators' response time, potentially affecting critical evidence preservation during initial crash site assessment.

The B-2 Spirit Bomber’s Flat Architecture Explained: Radar Evasion Tactics For Pilots

Inside America's tailless, flying wing stealth bomber.

The B-2's blended wing design eliminates traditional fuselage and tail surfaces, reducing radar cross-section to that of a small bird while maintaining subsonic efficiency and intercontinental range.

A flight attendant's miraculous survival after being thrown more than 100 meters from an Air Canada Express plane during a fatal crash at LGA.

Extreme survival cases like this highlight critical gaps in restraint systems and emergency protocols for flight crew positioned in vulnerable jump seats during catastrophic accidents.

Why Newer Aircraft Aren’t Always Better

The newest jets promise efficiency and luxury, but hidden trade-offs lurk beneath the shiny exterior.

Advanced avionics and materials in newer aircraft can introduce unfamiliar failure modes and higher maintenance complexity that older, battle-tested designs avoid.

Reno-Tahoe's Mustang 9 Departure requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural demands that differ from standard operations at runways 17L/R.

‘Cyber defense’ squadrons at Cape Canaveral and Vandenberg reflect growing concern over malicious hacks The post Space Force adds cyber units to guard rocket launches appeared first on SpaceNews.

Space Force's deployment of dedicated cyber defense squadrons signals that launch infrastructure is now a primary target for adversarial cyberattacks, requiring integrated security protocols beyond traditional physical perimeter defense.

NASA Data Hackathon Inspires Community Action

This initiative empowers libraries, ed...

NASA's data hackathon model demonstrates how open-sourcing satellite and environmental datasets can accelerate innovation beyond traditional aerospace applications into community problem-solving.

Amazon Leo targets faster deployment cadence as deadline pressure mounts

Amazon vowed to double the annual launch rate for its low Earth orbit broadband constellation to more than 20 missions, hinging largely on rockets yet to prove themselves at scale. The post Amazon Leo targets faster deployment cadence as deadline pressure mounts appeared first on SpaceNews.

Amazon's Leo constellation acceleration depends on unproven launch vehicles, creating supply chain risk for airlines and operators relying on satellite-based connectivity solutions.

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

Science Through Shadows: How Astronomical Alignments Reveal the Universe

When one celestial object passes in front of another, it can cast a shadow that travels across space – and sometimes across Earth. These moments of alignment, known as eclipses, occultations, and transits, allow scientists to study distant objects in remarkable ways. By observing how light change...

Astronomical alignment techniques used for celestial observation offer potential methodologies for satellite tracking and space situational awareness in aviation operations.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 24, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation segments—not just aircraft technology—is essential for achieving sustainability goals amid projected traffic growth.

See MDPI →

Hybrid antenna developed by Parsons and Raven Defense combines a six-meter parabolic dish with an electronically steered phased-array feed The post Parsons unveils new satellite antenna aimed at military market appeared first on SpaceNews.

The hybrid antenna's combination of traditional parabolic and phased-array technologies enables simultaneous multi-beam operations, potentially reducing military satellite communication latency and increasing operational flexibility.

Military Mar 23, 2026

Remembering the Green Ramp Disaster

Tragedy struck on March 23, 1994, at Pope Air Force Base, North Carolina, on an aircraft parking ramp known as ‘Green Ramp.’ A number of paratroopers were lost and several injured after flames and debris rained down on their training and assembly area after a mid-air collision between a fighter j...

The 1994 Green Ramp disaster killed 24 paratroopers when a fighter jet collided with a transport plane overhead, highlighting critical airspace management risks near military training operations.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

See NASA’s GUARDIAN Catch a Tsunami

A new data visualization illustrates how an experimental NASA technology can provide extra lead time to communities in the path of a tsunami. Called GUARDIAN (GNSS Upper Atmospheric Real-time Disaster Information and Alert Network), the software detects slight distortions in satellite navigation ...

NASA's GUARDIAN system detects tsunami precursor signals through satellite navigation distortions, potentially extending warning time for coastal communities beyond traditional detection methods.

See NASA →
